title: carpophora viscosa
intro: Carpophora viscosa, also known as sticky bush, is a species of flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. Carpophora viscosa can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to soothe and protect the skin.
